faq.question: How much does window replacement cost in San Jose?
faq.answer: San Jose homeowners typically invest $6,000 to $16,000 for a full window replacement project covering 10–16 windows. Silicon Valley's largest city offers enormous variety — from modest 1960s ranches in Berryessa ($6,000–$10,000) to premium Willow Glen Craftsmans and Almaden Valley estates ($12,000–$22,000+). Single window installation starts at approximately $520.

faq.question: Do I need a permit for window replacement in San Jose?
faq.answer: Yes. San Jose requires a building permit for window replacements. The permitting process varies by district; historic homes in Willow Glen and the Rose Garden may face additional review. Our team manages all San Jose permit applications, inspections, and any required historic review on your behalf.

faq.question: Which window brands does Deluxe Windows install in San Jose?
faq.answer: We tailor brand recommendations to each San Jose neighborhood. In Willow Glen and Rose Garden, we frequently install Andersen 400-Series for period-appropriate aesthetics. In Evergreen, Almaden, and Silicon Valley tech corridors, Anlin Del Mar triple-pane is a top choice for energy efficiency. Milgard Tuscany vinyl is the standard for cost-effective whole-home replacements citywide.

faq.question: Does Deluxe Windows serve all San Jose neighborhoods?
faq.answer: Yes — we serve all San Jose districts including Willow Glen, Rose Garden, Evergreen, Almaden Valley, Berryessa, Silver Creek, Alum Rock, Cambrian, Blossom Hill, and all zip codes: 95101–95173.
